summary: Four strong lines survive and are distinct enough to justify a real multi-family campaign: one harness-reset family, one executable selector family, and two honest deferred program families. Most other candidates are children, narrower variants, or weaker duplicates of those four.
global_warnings: The batch contains heavy duplication around three themes: metric-lane reset, artifact selection, and controller/branch programs.

Several `catalog_executable_now` claims overreach the evidence. State-conditioned controller or ownership-transfer lines should stay blocked unless the active pr1394 harness exposes those primitives explicitly.
Any proposal that admits training-side families by pre-quant ranking violates the stated campaign lane. Promotion must stay on downstream `quantized_sliding_window` `score_bpb`.
feedback_to_generator: Next round, submit one best exemplar per mechanism family instead of four paraphrases. Keep downstream `score_bpb` as the campaign admission/promotion law, use pre-quant only as diagnostic or kill-guard evidence, and stop laundering local children like EMA-only selectors or recurrence ramps into separate ontology families. If the catalog does not already expose a primitive, mark the family blocked and define the primitive backlog explicitly.

reasons: This is the right search level, but it is a weaker version of the stronger control-paired downstream admission family.
Downstream-only ranking by itself does not solve pack-specific noise or control spread, so it is not yet the sharpest harness probe.
rewrite_instructions: Rewrite upward to a control-paired downstream admission family.
Make the smallest probe an 8-slot pack with replay-equivalent control clones and promote only on improvement over the control-noise band.
Keep pre-quant metrics strictly diagnostic, not part of admission ranking.

reasons: A recurrence ramp is still mostly a shaped retune of the existing recurrence schedule.
It is a child of the stronger recurrence-handoff story rather than a distinct first-order family.
rewrite_instructions: Move up one search level from ramp shape to recurrence handoff program.
Break the invariant that recurrence timing should be a fixed fraction at all.
Use a decisive probe with fixed control, coarse late handoff, and signal-triggered handoff, all judged by downstream `score_bpb`.

reasons: EMA-only selection is too narrow to stand as a top-level family.
It is a local child of the broader checkpoint/export selector mechanism.
rewrite_instructions: Promote this to a full same-trace checkpoint-and-export selector family.
Search over a bounded late checkpoint window plus a fixed export menu, not just EMA decays.
Use downstream `score_bpb` to choose the winner and test whether the non-default artifact wins beyond control spread.

reasons: The mechanism is consequential, but the executable-now claim is not supported by the provided catalog evidence.
The previous lane feedback also shows this family was not framed cleanly for the campaign lane.
rewrite_instructions: If mixed-precision per-tensor export is not already in the active harness, mark this family blocked.
Restate it as a deployment-path family with one explicit new primitive: bounded per-matrix bit allocation under fixed bytes.
Use a same-checkpoint export bakeoff against uniform-bit controls on downstream `score_bpb`.

reasons: Artifact choice is a real family, but this version overreaches into selector heuristics that are not clearly exposed by the current harness.
It is broader and less honest than the cleaner checkpoint/export selector keep.
rewrite_instructions: Collapse this into a bounded checkpoint-and-export selector family with a small fixed menu.
Do not rely on unproven selector heuristics like late-slope or quant-proxy unless the harness already computes them.
Keep the probe same-trace and downstream-only.

reasons: The search unit is right, but the family is too generic and overlaps with stronger pr1394-specific transition lines.
It should be anchored to a concrete broken invariant in the target script, not a general controller substrate.
rewrite_instructions: Specialize this into one pr1394-specific handoff family, preferably recurrence or warmdown transition control.
Keep it blocked unless the active harness exposes state-conditioned controller primitives.
Use one explicit trigger program family rather than a broad menu of three-phase controllers.

reasons: This is a real consequential direction, but it is a looser version of the stronger shared-trunk late-branch portfolio keep.
The probe needs a tighter compute-accounting contract.
rewrite_instructions: Rewrite as a blocked shared-trunk late-branch portfolio family with marginal downstream gain per extra finisher budget as the key observable.
Make the decisive probe compare shared-trunk branches against a single-path control under matched extra late-step budget.
State the required branch-resume primitive explicitly.

reasons: This violates the stated campaign lane by admitting training families on pre-quant evidence.
It diagnoses a real problem, but the proposed fix reintroduces lane mixing instead of removing it.
rewrite_instructions: Keep the campaign admission and promotion metric on downstream `quantized_sliding_window` `score_bpb`.
Rewrite the family as downstream-only control-paired admission, using pre-quant only for diagnostics or early kill guards.
Measure success by improved survivor stability over control spread, not by split ranking laws.

reasons: The controller family is consequential, but the executable-now claim is too aggressive given the evidence that controller/search primitives are stranded or unproven in the active runner.
As written, it risks hiding missing machinery behind a generic controller label.
rewrite_instructions: Mark this family blocked unless the pr1394 harness already exposes state-conditioned control hooks.
Anchor it to one concrete transition mechanism in the target script rather than a general phase-controller umbrella.
Use a minimal three-way probe: frozen control, coarse rule handoff, signal-triggered handoff.

reasons: Ownership-transfer language is promising, but in this form it is too close to a bundle of optimizer-role retunes.
The executable-now claim is not credible without explicit harness support for handoff states or role programs.
rewrite_instructions: Move up from ownership rhetoric to a formal blocked handoff-program family with explicit states, triggers, and actions.
Define the broken invariant as static optimizer-role allocation across phases.
Use one bounded bootstrap->receiver program and matched static controls as the decisive probe.

reasons: This is the cleanest artifact-selection family in the batch: the searchable object is explicit and first-order.
The same-trace checkpoint/export bakeoff gives a crisp falsifier with no lane mixing.
It stays close to currently plausible harness behavior because it only needs a bounded late checkpoint window and fixed export menu.
This is a real consequential family, but the signal-triggered version is one level too specific before the branch primitive exists.
It should first survive as a shared-trunk branch portfolio family, then specialize to run-state triggers later.
rewrite_instructions: Rewrite upward to a blocked shared-trunk late-branch portfolio family.
Break the invariant that one committed finisher is the right compute topology; do not lead with stranded trigger signals.
After branch primitives exist, reintroduce trigger logic as within-family mutation.

reasons: This is the strongest harness-reset line because it changes the unit of comparison, not the model.
Control-paired delta admission is the right answer to pack-specific noise and directly serves the downstream-only campaign law.
The probe is cheap, decisive, and executable without inventing new model-side machinery.
This is a good selector family, but it is narrower and weaker than the stronger checkpoint-and-export selector keep.
Restricting the family to checkpoint choice under a fixed deployment path leaves downstream deployment variation on the table.
rewrite_instructions: Promote this to checkpoint-and-export selection on the same training trace.
Keep the selector bounded: a small late checkpoint window and a fixed export menu.
Judge only on downstream `score_bpb`, with pre-quant as secondary evidence.

reasons: This is the strongest transition-program family because it attacks a concrete invariant in the target script: fixed recurrence activation timing.
It moves recurrence from scalar tuning to a true handoff policy.
The family is honest about needing new runtime state-conditioned control hooks, which makes it a valid deferred keep rather than fake executability.
This is a real composition-level move that changes compute topology, not just a finisher tweak.
It is distinct from selector-only families because it reallocates training budget into a shared-trunk portfolio.
The blocked status is honest and the decisive probe is still interpretable once branch-resume machinery exists.
